title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
text: Note: /tables/countries endpoint accept language parameter. Country names returned Portuguese. Get detailed info specific country:
code: library(comexr)  # Full list (281 entries) countries <- comex_countries() countries #> # A tibble: 281 × 2 #>    id    text #>    <chr> <chr> #>  1 994   A Designar #>  2 132   Afeganistão #>  3 175   Albânia #>  ...  # Search by name comex_countries(search = "United") #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>    id    text #>    <chr> <chr> #>  1 249   Estados Unidos #>  2 ... comex_country_detail(105) #> $id #> [1] "105" #> $country #> [1] "Brasil" #> $coPaisIson3 #> [1] "076" #> $coPaisIsoa3 #> [1] "BRA"

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
text: add parameter can request additional columns (API-dependent):
code: # In Portuguese comex_blocs(language = "pt") #> # A tibble: 12 × 2 #>    id    text #>    <chr> <chr> #>  1 105   América Central e Caribe #>  2 107   América do Norte #>  3 48    América do Sul #>  ...  # In English comex_blocs(language = "en") comex_blocs(language = "en", add = TRUE)

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
code: states <- comex_states() states #> # A tibble: 34 × 3 #>    text              id    uf #>    <chr>             <chr> <chr> #>  1 Acre              12    AC #>  2 Alagoas           27    AL #>  3 Amapá             16    AP #>  ...  # Detail for a specific state comex_state_detail(26) #> $coUf #> [1] "26" #> $sgUf #> [1] "PE" #> $noUf #> [1] "Pernambuco" #> $noRegiao #> [1] "REGIAO NORDESTE"

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
code: # Full list (5,570 municipalities) cities <- comex_cities() cities #> # A tibble: 5,570 × 3 #>    id      text                   noMunMin #>    <chr>   <chr>                  <chr> #>  1 5300050 Abadia de Goiás - GO   Abadia de Goiás #>  ...  # Detail for a specific city comex_city_detail(2611606) #> $coMunGeo #> [1] "2611606" #> $noMun #> [1] "RECIFE" #> $noMunMin #> [1] "Recife" #> $sgUf #> [1] "PE"

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
code: comex_transport_modes() #> # A tibble: 17 × 2 #>    id    text #>    <chr> <chr> #>  1 00    VIA NAO DECLARADA #>  2 01    MARITIMA #>  3 02    FLUVIAL #>  4 04    AEREA #>  5 05    POSTAL #>  ...  # Note: use string codes with leading zeros comex_transport_mode_detail("01") #> $coVia #> [1] "01" #> $noVia #> [1] "MARITIMA"

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
code: customs <- comex_customs_units() customs #> # A tibble: 278 × 2 #>    id      text #>    <chr>   <chr> #>  1 0000000 0000000 - NAO INFORMADO #>  2 0117600 0117600 - ALF - PORTO DE MANAUS #>  ...  comex_customs_unit_detail(817600)

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
text: NCM primary product classification Brazilian trade since 1997. 8-digit code based Harmonized System (HS).
code: # Paginated — use page and per_page ncm <- comex_ncm(language = "en", page = 1, per_page = 10) ncm #> # A tibble: 10 × 3 #>    noNCM                       unit     coNcm #>    <chr>                       <chr>    <chr> #>  1 Adhesives based on rubber   KILOGRAM 35069110 #>  ...  # Search by keyword comex_ncm(language = "en", search = "soybean")  # Get detail for a specific NCM code comex_ncm_detail("02042200") #> $id #> [1] "02042200" #> $text #> [1] "Outras peças não desossadas de ovino, frescas ou refrigeradas"

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
code: nbm <- comex_nbm(language = "en", page = 1, per_page = 5) nbm #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>    nbm                                    coNbm #>    <chr>                                  <chr> #>  1 3-IODO-1-PROPENO (IODETO DE ALILA)    2903300305 #>  ...  comex_nbm_detail("2924101100") #> $coNBM #> [1] "2924101100" #> $noNBM #> [1] "ETILENO BIS ESTEARAMIDA"

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
text: HS table provides international product classification hierarchy: subheading (6-digit), heading (4-digit), chapter (2-digit). Note: HS table search parameter — language, add, page, per_page.
code: hs <- comex_hs(language = "en", page = 1, per_page = 5) hs #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>    subHeadingCode subHeading                     headingCode heading #>    <chr>          <chr>                          <chr>       <chr> #>  1 010110         Pure-bred breeding horses ...  0101        Live horses, ... #>  ...

what: CGCE — Classification by Broad Economic Categories (BEC)
title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
code: cgce <- comex_cgce(language = "en", page = 1, per_page = 5) cgce #> # A tibble: 5 × 6 #>    coBECLevel3 BECLevel3                       coBECLevel2 BECLevel2 #>    <chr>       <chr>                           <chr>       <chr> #>  1 110         Capital goods, except indust... 11          Capital goods, exce... #>  ...

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
text: SITC table 5 levels hierarchy: Section → Division → Group → Subgroup → Basic Heading.
code: sitc <- comex_sitc(language = "en", page = 1, per_page = 3) sitc #> # A tibble: 3 × 10 #>    coSITCBasicHeading SITCBasicHeading coSITCSubGroup SITCSubGroup ... #>    <chr>              <chr>            <chr>          <chr>        ... #>  1 I                  Gold, monetary   9710           Gold, non-m... ... #>  ...

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
text: Note: ISIC table uses endpoint SITC (/tables/product-categories). API currently provide separate ISIC-table.

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
text: main purpose tables find numeric codes use filters:
code: # 1. Find country code for Argentina countries <- comex_countries(search = "Argentina") # id = "021"  # 2. Find state code for Rio Grande do Sul states <- comex_states() # RS = id "43"  # 3. Query: Exports from RS to Argentina, grouped by HS4 result <- comex_export(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-12",   details      = c("state", "country", "hs4"),   filters      = list(     country = 21,     state   = 43   ) )

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
text: product tables thousands entries. Use page per_page paginate:
code: # Get all NCM codes (13,730 entries), 500 at a time all_ncm <- list() page <- 1  repeat {   batch <- comex_ncm(language = "en", page = page, per_page = 500)   if (nrow(batch) == 0) break   all_ncm[[page]] <- batch   page <- page + 1 }  all_ncm_df <- do.call(rbind, all_ncm) nrow(all_ncm_df) #> [1] 13730

title: Auxiliary Tables & Lookups
text: unsure filters details valid given endpoint, use discovery functions runtime: particularly useful API updated new fields metrics.
code: # What filters can I use for city queries? comex_filters("city")  # What values does filter "state" accept for city queries? comex_filter_values("state", type = "city")  # What metrics are available for historical queries? comex_metrics("historical")

title: Getting Started with comex
text: comex package provides R interface ComexStat API Brazilian Ministry Development, Industry, Trade Services (MDIC). gives programmatic access : General trade data — exports imports 1997 present, 22 detail/grouping fields 6 metrics. City-level trade data — trade statistics broken municipality. Historical data — export/import records 1989 1996 using older NBM nomenclature. Auxiliary lookup tables — countries, economic blocs, states, cities, transport modes, customs units, product nomenclatures (NCM, NBM, HS, CGCE, SITC). package two dependencies (httr2 cli) require headless browser external software.

title: Getting Started with comex
text: querying, can discover date range, available grouping fields (details), metrics data type:
code: library(comex)  # What is the most recent data available? comex_last_update("general") #> $updated #> [1] "2026-02-05" #> $year #> [1] "2026" #> $monthNumber #> [1] "01"  # What years are covered? comex_available_years("general") #> $max #> [1] "2026" #> $min #> [1] "1997"  # What detail/grouping fields can I use? comex_details("general") #> # A tibble: 22 × 2 #>    filter        text #>    <chr>         <chr> #>  1 country       Countries #>  2 economicBlock Economic Blocks #>  3 state         States #>  4 ...

title: Getting Started with comex
text: easiest way query comex_export() comex_import():
code: # Top export destinations in January 2024 exports <- comex_export(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-01",   details      = "country" )  exports #> # A tibble: 219 × 4 #>    year  country                   metricFOB metricKG #>    <chr> <chr>                         <dbl>    <dbl> #>  1 2024  China                    7812623070    ... #>  2 2024  United States            3254810234    ... #>  3 2024  Argentina                 916456789    ... #>  ...

title: Getting Started with comex
text: Filter codes come auxiliary tables — use comex_countries(), comex_states(), etc. look .
code: # Exports to China and USA, broken down by HS4 product group exports_filtered <- comex_export(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-06",   details      = c("country", "hs4"),   filters      = list(country = c(160, 249)),   month_detail = TRUE )

title: Getting Started with comex
code: # All countries countries <- comex_countries() countries #> # A tibble: 281 × 2 #>    id    text #>    <chr> <chr> #>  1 994   A Designar #>  2 132   Afeganistão #>  ...  # Search for a specific country comex_countries(search = "Brazil")

title: Getting Started with comex
text: details parameter accepts user-friendly names mapped API’s internal names. full list: Note: City endpoint uses heading (≈HS4), chapter (≈HS2), section — sh2/sh4/sh6. Use comex_details("city") confirm.

title: Getting Started with comex
text: functions accept language parameter. Use "pt" (Portuguese), "en" (English), "es" (Spanish):
code: comex_blocs(language = "pt") #> # A tibble: 12 × 2 #>    id    text #>    <chr> <chr> #>  1 105   América Central e Caribe #>  2 107   América do Norte #>  ...  comex_blocs(language = "en") #> # A tibble: 12 × 2 #>    id    text #>    <chr> <chr> #>  1 105   Central America and Caribbean #>  2 107   North America #>  ...

title: Getting Started with comex
text: See vignette("querying-trade-data") advanced query patterns including monthly breakdowns, multiple metrics, CIF values, working city historical endpoints. See vignette("auxiliary-tables") tour lookup tables (NCM, HS, countries, blocs, classifications).

title: Querying Trade Data
text: default, comex_export() returns FOB value (US$) net weight (kg). row combination requested detail fields year.
code: library(comexr)  exports <- comex_export(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-12",   details      = "country" ) #> ℹ Querying exports from 2024-01 to 2024-12 #> ✔ POST /general [580ms] #> ✔ 219 records found

title: Querying Trade Data
text: import endpoint supports additional metrics — freight, insurance, CIF:
code: imports <- comex_import(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-06",   details      = "country",   metric_cif   = TRUE ) # Returns columns: year, country, metricFOB, metricKG, metricCIF

title: Querying Trade Data
text: Set month_detail = TRUE get data split month:
code: monthly <- comex_export(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-06",   details      = "country",   month_detail = TRUE ) # Now each row also includes a month column

title: Querying Trade Data
text: Pass character vector details cross-tabulate:
code: by_country_product <- comex_export(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-03",   details      = c("country", "hs4"),   month_detail = TRUE ) # Rows grouped by: year × month × country × HS4 product heading

title: Querying Trade Data
text: Use filters restrict query specific values. Filter codes come auxiliary tables. Multiple filters can combined:
code: # Step 1: Find the codes for China and USA countries <- comex_countries(search = "China") # id = 160 (China), 249 (United States)  # Step 2: Query only exports to these countries to_china_usa <- comex_export(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-12",   details      = c("country", "section"),   filters      = list(country = c(160, 249)) ) # Exports of HS section 02 (Vegetable products) from São Paulo sp_veg <- comex_export(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-12",   details      = c("state", "hs2"),   filters      = list(state = 35, sh2 = "02") )

title: Querying Trade Data
text: full control, use comex_query() lets specify flow, metrics, options:
code: result <- comex_query(   flow         = "import",   start_period = "2023-01",   end_period   = "2023-12",   details      = c("country", "ncm"),   filters      = list(country = c(160)),   month_detail = FALSE,   metric_fob   = TRUE,   metric_kg    = TRUE,   metric_statistic = TRUE,   metric_freight   = TRUE,   metric_insurance = TRUE,   metric_cif       = TRUE,   language     = "en" )

title: Querying Trade Data
text: city endpoint provides municipal-level detail limited set grouping fields metrics. Important: city endpoint uses heading, chapter, section instead sh6/sh4/sh2. NCM classification details (CGCE, SITC, ISIC) available. CIF freight metrics available either.
code: # Which details are available for city queries? comex_details("city") #> # A tibble: 7 × 2 #>    filter        text #>    <chr>         <chr> #>  1 country       Countries #>  2 economicBlock Economic Blocks #>  3 state         States #>  4 city          Cities #>  5 heading       Headings #>  6 chapter       Chapters #>  7 section       Sections  # Which metrics? comex_metrics("city") #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>    id          text #>    <chr>       <chr> #>  1 metricFOB   US$ FOB #>  2 metricKG    Net Weight (KG) # Exports from Pernambuco (state 26) by country pe_exports <- comex_query_city(   flow         = "export",   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-12",   details      = c("state", "country"),   filters      = list(state = 26) )  # Exports from Recife (city 2611606) by product section recife <- comex_query_city(   flow         = "export",   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-06",   details      = c("city", "section"),   filters      = list(city = 2611606) )

title: Querying Trade Data
text: historical endpoint covers period current NCM nomenclature adopted. uses NBM (Nomenclatura Brasileira de Mercadorias) system.
code: comex_available_years("historical") #> $max #> [1] "1996" #> $min #> [1] "1989"  comex_details("historical") #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>    filter  text #>    <chr>   <chr> #>  1 country Countries #>  2 state   States #>  3 nbm     NBM #>  4 section Sections # Historical exports by country in 1990 hist_1990 <- comex_historical(   flow         = "export",   start_period = "1990-01",   end_period   = "1990-12",   details      = "country" )

title: Querying Trade Data
text: query functions return data.frame (tibble tibble package installed). Column names come directly API — detail/metric names requested.
code: library(dplyr)  # Top 10 export destinations in 2024 by FOB value top10 <- comex_export(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-12",   details      = "country" ) |>   arrange(desc(metricFOB)) |>   head(10)  # Monthly export trend to China china_monthly <- comex_export(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period   = "2024-12",   details      = "country",   filters      = list(country = 160),   month_detail = TRUE )

title: Querying Trade Data
text: Start discovery. Use comex_details(), comex_filters(), comex_metrics() building complex queries. Use filters reduce response size. API limit ~150,000 rows. query broad, API timeout. Add filters reduce date range. Filter codes integers. Use comex_countries(), comex_states(), comex_filter_values() find numeric codes. details parameter accepts user-friendly names. Write "hs4" instead "sh4", "transport_mode" instead "transportMode". package maps automatically. City endpoint fewer features. 7 details, 2 metrics, different product grouping names (heading/chapter/section). Historical endpoint uses NBM. "ncm" detail available — use "nbm" instead.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Andre Leite. Author, maintainer. Marcos Wasilew. Author. Hugo Vasconcelos. Author. Carlos Amorin. Author. Diogo Bezerra. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Leite , Wasilew M, Vasconcelos H, Amorin C, Bezerra D (2026). comexr: Client Brazilian Foreign Trade Statistics API ('ComexStat'). R package version 0.2.0, https://strategicprojects.github.io/comexr/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {comexr: Client for the Brazilian Foreign Trade Statistics API ('ComexStat')},   author = {Andre Leite and Marcos Wasilew and Hugo Vasconcelos and Carlos Amorin and Diogo Bezerra},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://strategicprojects.github.io/comexr/}, }

what: comexr - R Interface to Brazilian ComexStat API
title: comexr - R Interface to Brazilian ComexStat API
text: comexr package provides complete R interface ComexStat API Brazilian Ministry Development, Industry, Trade Services (MDIC). allows programmatic access detailed Brazilian export import data.

title: comexr - R Interface to Brazilian ComexStat API
text: 30 functions covering API endpoints General trade data (1997–present), city-level data, historical records (1989–1996) Auxiliary tables: countries, economic blocs, NCM/NBM/HS product codes, CGCE/SITC/ISIC classifications, states, cities, transport modes, customs units 2 dependencies: httr2 + cli Multilingual: Portuguese, English, Spanish SSL auto-fallback: handles ICP-Brasil certificate issues transparently

title: comexr - R Interface to Brazilian ComexStat API
comexr - R Interface to Brazilian ComexStat API
code: library(comexr)  # Exports by country in January 2024 (monthly detail by default) exports <- comex_export(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period = "2024-01",   details = "country" )  # Imports with CIF value imports <- comex_import(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period = "2024-12",   details = "country",   metric_cif = TRUE )  # Filter: exports to China (160), grouped by HS4 soy <- comex_export(   start_period = "2024-01",   end_period = "2024-12",   details = c("country", "hs4"),   filters = list(country = 160) )

title: comexr - R Interface to Brazilian ComexStat API
code: # What grouping fields are available? comex_details("general")  # What filters can I use? comex_filters("general")  # Look up country codes countries <- comex_countries() countries[grepl("China", countries$text, ignore.case = TRUE), ]  # Economic blocs in Portuguese comex_blocs(language = "pt")

title: comexr - R Interface to Brazilian ComexStat API
text: systems API’s ICP-Brasil certificate chain recognized. package handles automatically — first failure retries without SSL verification issues warning. suppress:

Get available years for queries — comex_available_years

Get economic blocs table — comex_blocs

text: language Language: "pt", "en", "es". Default: "en". search Optional search term filter results. add Optional related table include (e.g. "ncm"). page Page number pagination. Default: NULL (results). per_page Number results per page. Default: NULL. verbose Logical. Show progress messages. Default: FALSE.

text: data.frame available details.

title: Query exports — comex_export
text: Shortcut comex_query() flow = "export".

title: Query exports — comex_export
code: comex_export(   start_period,   end_period,   details = NULL,   filters = NULL,   month_detail = TRUE,   metric_fob = TRUE,   metric_kg = TRUE,   metric_statistic = FALSE,   metric_freight = FALSE,   metric_insurance = FALSE,   metric_cif = FALSE,   language = "en",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Query exports — comex_export
text: start_period Start period "YYYY-MM" format (e.g. "2023-01"). end_period End period "YYYY-MM" format (e.g. "2023-12"). details Character vector detail/grouping fields. Options: Geographic: "country", "bloc", "state", "city", "transport_mode", "customs_unit" Products: "ncm", "hs6" ("sh6"), "hs4" ("sh4"), "hs2" ("sh2"), "section" CGCE: "cgce_n1", "cgce_n2", "cgce_n3" SITC/CUCI: "sitc_section", "sitc_chapter", "sitc_position", "sitc_subposition", "sitc_item" ISIC: "isic_section", "isic_division", "isic_group", "isic_class" : "company_size" (imports ) filters Named list filters. Names match detail field names. Example: list(country = c(160, 249), state = c(26, 13)) month_detail Logical. TRUE, break results month. Default: FALSE. metric_fob Logical. Include FOB value (US$). Default: TRUE. metric_kg Logical. Include net weight (kg). Default: TRUE. metric_statistic Logical. Include statistical quantity. Default: FALSE. metric_freight Logical. Include freight value (US$, imports ). Default: FALSE. metric_insurance Logical. Include insurance value (US$, imports ). Default: FALSE. metric_cif Logical. Include CIF value (US$, imports ). Default: FALSE. language Response language: "pt", "en", "es". Default: "en". verbose Logical. Show progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Query exports — comex_export
text: data.frame (tibble) export data.

what: Query historical foreign trade data (1989-1996) — comex_historical
title: Query historical foreign trade data (1989-1996) — comex_historical
text: Query historical data endpoint ComexStat API retrieve Brazilian export import data 1989 1996, SISCOMEX system implemented. Historical data uses NBM (Brazilian Nomenclature Goods) classification.

title: Query historical foreign trade data (1989-1996) — comex_historical
code: comex_historical(   flow = "export",   start_period,   end_period,   details = NULL,   filters = NULL,   month_detail = TRUE,   metric_fob = TRUE,   metric_kg = TRUE,   language = "en",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Query historical foreign trade data (1989-1996) — comex_historical
text: flow Trade flow: "export" "import". start_period Start period "YYYY-MM" format (e.g. "1990-01"). end_period End period "YYYY-MM" format (e.g. "1996-12"). details Character vector detail/grouping fields. Options: "country", "state", "nbm". filters Named list filters. month_detail Logical. TRUE, break month. Default: TRUE. metric_fob Logical. Include FOB value (US$). Default: TRUE. metric_kg Logical. Include net weight (kg). Default: TRUE. language Response language: "pt", "en", "es". Default: "en". verbose Logical. Show progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Query historical foreign trade data (1989-1996) — comex_historical
text: data.frame (tibble) query results.

title: Query historical foreign trade data (1989-1996) — comex_historical
text: Historical data differs general data: Available period: 1989 1996 Limited details: "country", "state", "nbm" Product classification NBM (NCM) FOB KG metrics available (statistic, freight, insurance, CIF)

title: Query historical foreign trade data (1989-1996) — comex_historical
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Historical exports 1995-1996 by country comex_historical(   flow = "export",   start_period = "1995-01",   end_period = "1996-12",   details = "country" ) } # }

title: Get Harmonized System (HS) tables — comex_hs
text: Returns Harmonized System classification tables. HS international product nomenclature developed World Customs Organization (WCO).

title: Get Harmonized System (HS) tables — comex_hs
code: comex_hs(   language = "en",   add = NULL,   page = NULL,   per_page = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Get Harmonized System (HS) tables — comex_hs
text: language Language: "pt", "en", "es". Default: "en". add Optional related table include (e.g. "ncm"). page Page number pagination. Default: NULL. per_page Number results per page. Default: NULL. verbose Logical. Default: FALSE.

title: Get Harmonized System (HS) tables — comex_hs
text: data.frame HS codes descriptions.

title: Get Harmonized System (HS) tables — comex_hs
text: Harmonized System organized hierarchically: Section: 21 sections (broadest grouping) Chapter (HS2): ~97 chapters (2 digits) Heading (HS4): 4 digits Subheading (HS6): 6 digits (detailed) NCM adds 2 digits HS6 code.

title: Get Harmonized System (HS) tables — comex_hs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # All HS classifications comex_hs()  # With related NCM codes comex_hs(add = "ncm", per_page = 10) } # }

title: Query imports — comex_import
text: Shortcut comex_query() flow = "import".

title: Query imports — comex_import
code: comex_import(   start_period,   end_period,   details = NULL,   filters = NULL,   month_detail = TRUE,   metric_fob = TRUE,   metric_kg = TRUE,   metric_statistic = FALSE,   metric_freight = FALSE,   metric_insurance = FALSE,   metric_cif = FALSE,   language = "en",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Query imports — comex_import
text: start_period Start period "YYYY-MM" format (e.g. "2023-01"). end_period End period "YYYY-MM" format (e.g. "2023-12"). details Character vector detail/grouping fields. Options: Geographic: "country", "bloc", "state", "city", "transport_mode", "customs_unit" Products: "ncm", "hs6" ("sh6"), "hs4" ("sh4"), "hs2" ("sh2"), "section" CGCE: "cgce_n1", "cgce_n2", "cgce_n3" SITC/CUCI: "sitc_section", "sitc_chapter", "sitc_position", "sitc_subposition", "sitc_item" ISIC: "isic_section", "isic_division", "isic_group", "isic_class" : "company_size" (imports ) filters Named list filters. Names match detail field names. Example: list(country = c(160, 249), state = c(26, 13)) month_detail Logical. TRUE, break results month. Default: FALSE. metric_fob Logical. Include FOB value (US$). Default: TRUE. metric_kg Logical. Include net weight (kg). Default: TRUE. metric_statistic Logical. Include statistical quantity. Default: FALSE. metric_freight Logical. Include freight value (US$, imports ). Default: FALSE. metric_insurance Logical. Include insurance value (US$, imports ). Default: FALSE. metric_cif Logical. Include CIF value (US$, imports ). Default: FALSE. language Response language: "pt", "en", "es". Default: "en". verbose Logical. Show progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Query imports — comex_import
text: data.frame (tibble) import data.

title: Query imports — comex_import
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ comex_import(   start_period = "2023-01",   end_period = "2023-12",   details = "country",   metric_cif = TRUE ) } # }

what: Initialize session for API calls — comex_init
title: Initialize session for API calls — comex_init
text: Starts browser session using rvest's read_html_live() solves Cloudflare challenge. API calls made via session.

title: Initialize session for API calls — comex_init
Initialize session for API calls — comex_init
text: force TRUE, close existing session create new one. Default: FALSE

title: Initialize session for API calls — comex_init
text: Invisibly returns TRUE success

title: Initialize session for API calls — comex_init
text: function uses rvest::read_html_live() internally uses chromote create headless Chrome session. session navigates API domain solve Cloudflare's JavaScript challenge. session stays open reused subsequent API calls. Call comex_close() done free resources.

title: Initialize session for API calls — comex_init
text: Requires rvest package (>= 1.0.0) Chrome/Chromium browser.

title: Initialize session for API calls — comex_init
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Initialize session (called automatically on first API call) comex_init()  # Make API calls years <- comex_available_years()  # Close when done comex_close() } # }

Default: NULL. per_page Number results per page. Default: NULL. verbose Logical. Show progress messages. Default: FALSE.

title: Get ISIC (International Standard Industrial Classification) table — comex_isic
text: OpenAPI specification define dedicated ISIC table endpoint. ISIC codes available detail/grouping fields trade queries (e.g. "isic_section", "isic_division"). convenience function queries /tables/product-categories, may return ISIC data alongside CUCI/SITC classifications. can also look ISIC values using comex_filter_values() filter names like "isicSection".

title: Get ISIC (International Standard Industrial Classification) table — comex_isic
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Browse product categories (includes ISIC) comex_isic()  # Alternatively, look up ISIC values via filters: comex_filter_values("isicSection") } # }

what: Query general foreign trade data — comex_query
title: Query general foreign trade data — comex_query
text: Query main ComexStat API endpoint retrieve Brazilian export import data. Supports filtering grouping multiple classifications NCM, Harmonized System, countries, states, etc. Data available monthly 1997 recent complete month.

title: Query general foreign trade data — comex_query
code: comex_query(   flow = "export",   start_period,   end_period,   details = NULL,   filters = NULL,   month_detail = TRUE,   metric_fob = TRUE,   metric_kg = TRUE,   metric_statistic = FALSE,   metric_freight = FALSE,   metric_insurance = FALSE,   metric_cif = FALSE,   language = "en",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Query general foreign trade data — comex_query
text: flow Trade flow: "export" "import". start_period Start period "YYYY-MM" format (e.g. "2023-01"). end_period End period "YYYY-MM" format (e.g. "2023-12"). details Character vector detail/grouping fields. Options: Geographic: "country", "bloc", "state", "city", "transport_mode", "customs_unit" Products: "ncm", "hs6" ("sh6"), "hs4" ("sh4"), "hs2" ("sh2"), "section" CGCE: "cgce_n1", "cgce_n2", "cgce_n3" SITC/CUCI: "sitc_section", "sitc_chapter", "sitc_position", "sitc_subposition", "sitc_item" ISIC: "isic_section", "isic_division", "isic_group", "isic_class" : "company_size" (imports ) filters Named list filters. Names match detail field names. Example: list(country = c(160, 249), state = c(26, 13)) month_detail Logical. TRUE, break results month. Default: FALSE. metric_fob Logical. Include FOB value (US$). Default: TRUE. metric_kg Logical. Include net weight (kg). Default: TRUE. metric_statistic Logical. Include statistical quantity. Default: FALSE. metric_freight Logical. Include freight value (US$, imports ). Default: FALSE. metric_insurance Logical. Include insurance value (US$, imports ). Default: FALSE. metric_cif Logical. Include CIF value (US$, imports ). Default: FALSE. language Response language: "pt", "en", "es". Default: "en". verbose Logical. Show progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Query general foreign trade data — comex_query
text: data.frame (tibble available) query results.

title: Query general foreign trade data — comex_query
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Brazilian exports in 2023, by country comex_query(   flow = "export",   start_period = "2023-01",   end_period = "2023-12",   details = "country" )  # Imports 2023 by NCM + country, filtered by specific countries comex_query(   flow = "import",   start_period = "2023-01",   end_period = "2023-12",   details = c("ncm", "country"),   filters = list(country = c(160, 249)),   month_detail = TRUE,   metric_cif = TRUE ) } # }

what: Query city-level foreign trade data — comex_query_city
title: Query city-level foreign trade data — comex_query_city
text: Query city endpoint ComexStat API. City-level data aggregated general data, fewer available details metrics. City information based declarant exports/imports, producer buyer.

title: Query city-level foreign trade data — comex_query_city
code: comex_query_city(   flow = "export",   start_period,   end_period,   details = NULL,   filters = NULL,   month_detail = TRUE,   metric_fob = TRUE,   metric_kg = TRUE,   metric_statistic = FALSE,   language = "en",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Query city-level foreign trade data — comex_query_city
text: flow Trade flow: "export" "import". start_period Start period "YYYY-MM" format. end_period End period "YYYY-MM" format. details Character vector detail/grouping fields. Options: Geographic: "country", "state", "city" Products: "hs6" ("sh6"), "hs4" ("sh4"), "hs2" ("sh2"), "section" filters Named list filters. Example: list(city = "3550308", state = "26") month_detail Logical. TRUE, break month. Default: FALSE. metric_fob Logical. Include FOB value (US$). Default: TRUE. metric_kg Logical. Include net weight (kg). Default: TRUE. metric_statistic Logical. Include statistical quantity. Default: FALSE. language Response language: "pt", "en", "es". Default: "en". verbose Logical. Show progress messages. Default: TRUE.

title: Query city-level foreign trade data — comex_query_city
text: data.frame (tibble) query results.

title: Query city-level foreign trade data — comex_query_city
text: City-level data differs general data: Full NCM available (use HS6/SH4/SH2) Classifications like CGCE, SITC, ISIC available FOB, KG, Statistical quantity metrics available Freight, Insurance, CIF metrics available

title: Query city-level foreign trade data — comex_query_city
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Exports from Pernambuco in 2023 comex_query_city(   flow = "export",   start_period = "2023-01",   end_period = "2023-12",   details = c("country", "state"),   filters = list(state = 26) ) } # }

what: comexr: R Client for the ComexStat API — comexr-package
title: comexr: R Client for the ComexStat API — comexr-package
text: comexr package provides complete R interface ComexStat API Brazilian Ministry Development, Industry, Trade Services (MDIC). API provides Brazilian foreign trade data 1997 (general data) 1989 (historical data), along auxiliary tables product nomenclatures, countries, economic classifications, .

title: comexr: R Client for the ComexStat API — comexr-package
text: functions query aggregated export import data configurable filters, details, metrics: comex_query(): Query general foreign trade data comex_export(): Shortcut export queries comex_import(): Shortcut import queries comex_query_city(): Query city-level data comex_historical(): Query historical data (1989-1996)

title: comexr: R Client for the ComexStat API — comexr-package
text: Functions retrieve information API : comex_last_update(): Date last data update comex_available_years(): Available years queries comex_filters(): Available filters comex_filter_values(): Possible values filter comex_details(): Available detail/grouping fields comex_metrics(): Available metrics

title: comexr 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-10

title: comexr 0.2.0
text: comex_historical() longer accepts metric_statistic, metric_freight, metric_insurance, metric_cif parameters. historical API endpoint supports FOB KG metrics. month_detail default changed FALSE TRUE query functions.

title: comexr 0.2.0
text: Fixed bloc detail mapping: now correctly maps economicBlock (API name). Added missing detail mappings: heading, chapter (city endpoint), nbm (historical endpoint), economic_block (alias). Fixed comex_metrics("general") returning 0 rows depends field contained NULL values. Fixed comex_ncm_detail(), comex_nbm_detail(), detail functions returning empty lists instead named lists. Fixed comex_historical() endpoint path (added required trailing slash). Fixed response_to_tibble() failing responses containing NULL nested list values row fields.

title: comexr 0.2.0
text: SSL auto-fallback: package now detects SSL certificate failures (common ICP-Brasil chain) automatically retries without verification, issuing one-time warning. Set options(comex.ssl_verifypeer = FALSE) suppress.

title: comexr 0.2.0
text: Corrected comex_query_city() documentation: city endpoint uses heading, chapter, section — hs4, hs2, hs6. Corrected comex_historical() documentation: country, state, nbm details available (ncm section). Updated comex_filter_values() documentation actual filters value endpoints (6 general, 7 city, 2 historical). Renamed package comex comexr.

title: comexr 0.1.0
text: 30 exported functions covering ComexStat API endpoints. General trade queries: comex_query(), comex_export(), comex_import(). City-level queries: comex_query_city(). Historical queries (1989-1996): comex_historical(). Metadata: comex_last_update(), comex_available_years(), comex_filters(), comex_filter_values(), comex_details(), comex_metrics(). Auxiliary tables geography, products, classifications.
